title: calea stenophylla
intro: Calea stenophylla, also known as Mexican Damiana or Slippery Elm, is a flowering plant native to Mexico and Central America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, such as alleviating digestive issues, anxiety, and insomnia, as well as its ability to boost energy and cognitive function. Calea stenophylla is often prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies, such as infusions and salves.
